TextENGNew: In the end, all the onigiri had been eaten by the principal, so I went without lunch.
Skipping lunch... I never knew the afternoon could stretch so long and so painfully.
Something other than drowsiness was making my consciousness drift away.
``A-Are you all right, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS19/02/500200051. ...That's why I told you to eat Rena's curry~.``
changeReason: Old says 'you shouldn't eat Rena's curry' but JPN 食べなよ means 'eat it!' — the advice is reversed.

TextENGNew:
As if I could eat that salt-laden, blood-pressure-spiking curry!
...Is what I said as I refused it.

I had no choice but to regret it.
...The hunger... and the stomach cramps......

"Well well!
You hung in there nicely.
In that absolutely hopeless situation, no less!
Kei-chan, you're really something when your back is against the wall!"

"...I don't need compliments right now.
...Just give me food~..."
"There'll be something at home to eat, right?"

"...We don't keep snacks around at home.
And I'm all out of cup noodles at the moment..."

"Ahahahaha!
If you tough it out and wait, I'm sure tonight's dinner will taste wonderful."
I had absolutely no desire to laugh along.

"Well, I'll take my leave then.
I have my part-time job again today.
...*sigh*, the work is rough when you're not used to it."
Ah, right — that shift at Angel Mort family restaurant.
...That uniform (?) was somehow rather... ☆ Hau~.
"...Being a waitress really is hard work."

"Eh? Mii-chan is working as a waitress?
Really? Really?!
Which restaurant?! Hau~~!!"

"N-No... I'm... right, I'm minding the toy shop from the other day!!
Shion is the waitress......"
Ah, right.
...I had completely forgotten that was how it was supposed to be.

"S-Sorry, sorry.
You two look so alike I got confused."

"Keiichi-kun.
...Who is Shion?"
Rena voiced the obvious question.

"Ah, well... Mion's younger twin sister.
Different personality, but they look exactly alike."

"That's right!
We look similar, but inside we're completely different!
I'm kind and thoughtful, while Shion has a cold and frightening personality...!"
"...I think when you two were born, all of Mion's girlish qualities went to Shion."
"...Unlike Mion, she's a genuinely bashful, lovely young woman with a smile to match."
changeReason: Old uses 'cheerful' but JPN says はにかんだ笑顔 (bashful smile); new correctly uses 'bashful,' which has a meaningfully different connotation—shy and demure rather than outgoing.

TextENGNew: "Ke-Ke-Kei-chaaaaAAAAaaan...!!!"
Rena listened to this exchange with her eyes blinking wide.
changeReason: Old says Rena 'interjected' and her eyes 'darted back and forth'; JPN says she was 聞き入っている (listening intently) with blinking eyes (ぱちくり). New correctly captures the listening and blinking.

TextENGNew:
"...Hmm...
Is that so?
Rena has never met her...
Mii-chan, did you ever tell me you had a younger sister?"
Shion's existence was already in danger of being exposed.

...I-I was in no position to cover for her...
Mion would have to handle this herself.

"Ah... yeah.
I suppose I never did introduce her to you, Rena."
"Ahaha, my twin sister's name is Shion."
changeReason: Old says just 'my younger sister'; new correctly includes 'twin' (双子), an important detail Mion reveals here.

TextENGNew:
"I didn't know.
I've never heard of her.
I never met her when I visited Mii-chan's house either."

"Erm... well, that!
You know I'm the only one who lives with Grandma.
Shion lives at the family home in Okinomiya.
W-We're not that close... so yeah, she doesn't really come out here~!"
Rena's expression hovered somewhere between convinced and unconvinced.
...She usually seemed like a bit of an airhead, but she had a keen instinct for these kinds of moments.
"Um... well... it's not a lie. Please believe us."
"Yeah, I believe you" — saying that, a bright smile suddenly bloomed on Rena's face.

"Rena would love to meet her someday~!
I'll go and see her for myself!
Which restaurant is she working at?! Which one?!"
"Umm...... that is... well........"

...Ah, she was getting more tangled by the moment.
I could see Mion's panic growing.
...I wanted to throw her a lifeline, but family matters were out of bounds for an outsider.

"Ah, oh! Sorry!!
I have to rush or I'll be late for work!!
See you!
Rena, Kei-chan!
See you tomorrow~!!"
Cutting the conversation short without ceremony, Mion set off running for home.

No matter how you looked at it, she was fleeing the scene.
We'll file this under emergency escape.

"Mii-chan was kind of adorable.
I wonder why, I wonder why.
Hehe...!"
Rena giggled in quiet amusement.

When would dinner be, I wondered...
Lying flat would probably burn the fewest calories.
I collapsed face-down in the entryway without even taking my shoes off.

Just as I was letting myself drift away, the doorbell rang.
I appeared to have a visitor.

"Come on in~.
It's open~......"
I directed my feeble voice at the door.

"...G-Good afternoon... huh?!
Kei-chan, what are you doing?!"
My visitor was Mion — or rather, not Mion at all, but Shion.
She looked startled to find me sprawled out in the entryway.

"I-Is that Shion...?!
But... why..."
I had thought Shion was an illusion that only existed at the restaurant, conjured up by Mion in that setting.
It had never occurred to me that Shion might show up here of her own accord.

"I heard from my sister.
Because of club activities today, you weren't able to eat lunch, were you?"

"...Yeah, it was quite an ordeal.
Satoko knocked over my pot, and then you — or someone — stole my rice..."
"W-Well, I'm... Shion..."

She turned red and sulked.
...Right, she was supposed to be the twin sister right now.

"Sorry, sorry.
...So what brings you here?
Did you bring me something to eat since I'm starving?"
"Oh my... ...It's a bit disappointing that you guessed it straight away, but — here."
With a crestfallen little smile, Shion held out the small bento box she had been hiding behind her back.

"Huh?!
Y-You really brought food?!
Th-Thanks!"

"There were leftovers from the hamburger steak my sister brought to school, so I brought them over.
I knew it would be close to dinner time... but I thought you might want them.
Am I imposing?"
changeReason: Old translates ハンバーグ as 'ground beef' (raw ingredient); new correctly uses 'hamburger steak,' which is the actual Japanese dish (a cooked meat patty).

TextENGNew:
"N-Not in the slightest...!
I'm genuinely happy!!
Can I really have this?!"
I lifted the lid and took a peek... and to call it 'a few leftovers' was far too modest. Inside was a beautifully arranged, careful little meal.

"I-Is it really all right?!
This isn't some prank where you've mixed in hot sauce or something?!"

"Geez.
I'm not my sister. I would never do something like that.
...If you don't want it, you don't have to force yourself...
I'll just take it home and eat it myself."
Saying that, she pouted with adorable petulance and made to snatch the box back.
...There were moments when I genuinely doubted she and Mion were the same person, and this was undoubtedly one of them.

"I-I want it very much.
I intend to eat every bite with heartfelt gratitude...!"
"I-It's... not something that warrants this much gratitude, to the point of tears..."
Shion, who had perhaps feared I might not accept the bento, looked down with a quietly happy expression.

"Th-There's not much on offer, but would you like to come in...?
I could probably manage some tea..."

"...Mm... sorry, some other time perhaps.
I'm on my way to work."

Oh, that's right.
She had said so.
"Just give the box a rinse and return it to my sister tomorrow, please."

"O-Of course!
After being allowed to eat something that looks this good, that's the very least I could do!"

"Well, I'll be off then.
.........Ah... ...One last thing..."
Shion went red again and looked down once more.

"...During that curry contest, my sister took your rice without asking, didn't she?
...When she gets carried away with a prank, she tends to go too far.
She didn't mean any real harm by it..."

"Hey now.
I don't mind about that at all.
There are no hard feelings when it comes to club activities!"

"In fact, I'm the one who should be grateful for all the excitement and entertainment every time!
...Could you pass that on to Mion for me?"

"...Yes.
...I think... my sister will be very glad to hear that.
Well, I must go...!"
Shion's face lit up with a bright, open smile.
She made a deep bow, then glanced at her watch and set off at a run.
"...I'm happy, but... is this really all right?"
I looked at the bento box she had left behind.
A faint warmth spread through the palm of my hand.
If it were Mion... I could picture hot sauce, mustard, maybe even a sewing needle inside. (Though surely she wouldn't actually go as far as a needle...)
I opened the box again and tasted a small piece hesitantly.

...I took a bite.
Then another.
...There was nothing amiss.
Nothing dangerous whatsoever — and it was delicious.

That's right.
This was Shion, not Mion. There had never been any cause for worry.
I hurried back to my room and ate with gratitude.
...I was so hungry that it was good enough to bring tears to my eyes.
"...Shion, ...no... Mion — hearing that I was practically dying of hunger, she rushed this over to me."

It was delicious to the very last bite.
...And nothing strange had been mixed in, not even at the end.
I felt a little embarrassed at myself for having been so suspicious.
